user_text:
The process begins with two possible starting points. First, a fault detector reports a failure to the Maintenance and Production Operator (MPO). Alternatively, the MPO may have a suspicion of their own fault. Regardless of which starting point occurs, the process converges at a decision point where the MPO examines the failure.

After examining the failure, the MPO faces another decision. If the MPO rejects the failure of the fault detector, the process moves directly to the end. However, if the MPO confirms the failure of the fault detector, several actions follow.

First, the MPO informs the General Operator (GO) about the confirmed failure. Then, the MPO also informs the Maintenance Service Provider (MSP). Following these notifications, the MPO proceeds to fix the fault at the measuring device. Once the repairs are completed, the MPO shares the results of the repairs.

After sharing the repair results, the MPO informs the GO about the resolution of the interference. Subsequently, the MPO also informs the MSP about the resolution of the interference. Once these notifications are completed, the process reaches its conclusion.
output_text:
The process begins when an event occurs, which can be either a fault detector reporting a failure to the Maintenance and Planning Officer (MPO) or the MPO having a suspicion of their own fault. These two possibilities are considered separately at the start.

If the fault detector reports a failure, the process moves to the point where the MPO examines the failure. Similarly, if the MPO has a suspicion of their own fault, the process also leads to the MPO examining the failure. This examination step is crucial as it determines the next actions.

Once the MPO examines the failure, two parallel paths are taken simultaneously. On one path, the MPO may reject the failure of the fault detector, indicating that the fault detector's report was incorrect or not applicable. This path does not lead to any further actions and eventually converges with the other path.

On the other path, the MPO confirms the failure of the fault detector. Upon confirmation, the MPO informs the General Operator (GO) about the situation. Following this, the MPO also informs the Maintenance Service Provider (MSP) about the failure. After these notifications, the MPO proceeds to fix the fault at the measuring device.

Once the fault is fixed, the MPO shares the results of the repairs. This involves informing the GO about the resolution of the interference caused by the fault. Subsequently, the MPO also informs the MSP about the resolution of the interference.

After all these steps are completed, the two parallel paths converge, and the process comes to an end.
